claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! It's nice to meet you. Let me ask a few questions about Alex to help determine your eligibility.

How old is Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to check a few more details.

Child Benefit can sometimes be claimed for children aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. However, at 21, Alex is above this age range.

Could you tell me if Alex is currently in any form of education or training? And if so, when did they start this course?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education. They work full-time actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to make an eligibility assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information from the official government guidance, I now have enough information to provide you with a determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 21, working full-time):**
- Child Benefit is normally available for children **under 16 years old**
- It can continue for children **under 20** only if they remain in approved education or training
- At 21 years old, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit
- Additionally, since Alex works full-time and is not in education or training, they would not qualify even if they were younger

I'm sorry I couldn't bring you better news. If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you have other children), please don't hesitate to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and works full-time. Child Benefit eligibility requires the child to be either: (1) under 16 years old, or (2) under 20 years old AND staying in approved education or training. At 21, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit of under 20 for those in approved education/training. Additionally, even if Alex were under 20, they would not qualify because they work full-time rather than being in approved education or training. The rules also state that Child Benefit stops if the child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, Alex does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and works full-time. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those under 20 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is over the age limit and is not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and works full-time. Child Benefit eligibility requires the child to be either: (1) under 16 years old, or (2) under 20 years old AND staying in approved education or training. At 21, Alex exceeds the maximum age limit of under 20 for those in approved education/training. Additionally, even if Alex were under 20, they would not qualify because they work full-time rather than being in approved education or training. The rules also state that Child Benefit stops if the child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Therefore, Alex does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and works full-time. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those under 20 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is over the age limit and is not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
